title: Cross-fitted super-learner blend with beta calibration on logits
summary: For AUC tasks, monotone calibration alone does not help a single model, but cross-fitted blending of heterogeneous models often does.
rationale: For AUC tasks, monotone calibration alone does not help a single model, but cross-fitted blending of heterogeneous models often does. Super Learner formalizes OOF-weighted ensembling, and beta calibration is a safer parametric map than plain Platt scaling when score distributions are skewed.
implementation_hint: Train a small library with genuinely different biases, e.g. current feature-engineered GBDT, lag-feature GBDT, CatBoost with native categoricals, and maybe a lightweight sequence model if available. Generate strict OOF logits, fit a non-negative meta-learner on OOF predictions, and optionally beta-calibrate each base model before blending or calibrate the final blend on a held-out fold only.
expected_effect: Usually small but real AUC gains from diversity and better ordering in the mid-probability region; also stabilizes leaderboard variance.
risk: If the base models are too correlated or calibration is fit on the same folds used for training predictions, the stack will overfit and add nothing.
